values.prompt:
def is_prime(n):
    """Return true if a given number is prime, and false otherwise.
    >>> is_prime(6)
    False
    >>> is_prime(101)
    True
    >>> is_prime(11)
    True
    >>> is_prime(13441)
    True
    >>> is_prime(61)
    True
    >>> is_prime(4)
    False
    >>> is_prime(1)
    False
    """

metadata.canonical_solution:
if n <= 1: return False
    n_sqrt = 1
    while n_sqrt ** 2 < n: n_sqrt += 1
    for i in range(2, min(n_sqrt + 1, n)):
        if n % i == 0:
            return False
    return True

values.description:
[[ ## description ## ]]
Function is_prime(n) returns False if n <= 1. It finds the smallest integer n_sqrt such that n_sqrt squared is at least n. Then it checks divisibility by each integer i from 2 up to min(n_sqrt, n-1). If any i divides n, returns False; otherwise returns True.
